name: Rama
description: Prince of Ayodhya, avatar of Vishnu, hero of the Ramayana

name: Sita
description: Wife of Rama, daughter of King Janaka

name: Lakshmana
description: Younger brother of Rama, loyal companion

name: Hanuman
description: Monkey god, devoted servant of Rama

name: Ravana
description: Demon king of Lanka, main antagonist

name: Dasaratha
description: King of Ayodhya, father of Rama

name: Kausalya
description: First queen of Dasaratha, mother of Rama

name: Kaikeyi
description: Second queen of Dasaratha, mother of Bharata

name: Sumitra
description: Third queen of Dasaratha, mother of Lakshmana and Shatrughna

name: Bharata
description: Son of Dasaratha and Kaikeyi, brother of Rama

name: Shatrughna
description: Son of Dasaratha and Sumitra, twin of Lakshmana

name: Vishvamitra
description: Sage who takes Rama to fight demons

name: Vashishtha
description: Royal guru of Ayodhya

name: Janaka
description: King of Mithila, father of Sita

name: Sugriva
description: Monkey king of Kishkindha, ally of Rama

name: Vali
description: Monkey king, brother of Sugriva

name: Jatayu
description: Vulture king who fights Ravana

name: Vibhishana
description: Brother of Ravana, defected to Rama

name: Indrajit
description: Son of Ravana, powerful warrior

name: Kumbhakarna
description: Giant brother of Ravana

name: Maricha
description: Demon who took the form of golden deer

name: Surpanakha
description: Ravana's sister, demoness

name: Mandodari
description: Wife of Ravana

name: Trijata
description: Rakshasi who guarded Sita in Lanka

name: Angada
description: Son of Vali, warrior in Rama's army

name: Jambavan
description: Bear king, advisor in Rama's army

name: Nala
description: Vanara engineer who built the bridge to Lanka

name: Nila
description: Vanara commander in Rama's army

name: Sampati
description: Brother of Jatayu, pointed vanaras to Lanka

name: Tataka
description: Demoness killed by Rama in the forest

name: Subahu
description: Demon killed by Rama during Vishvamitra's sacrifice

name: Parashurama
description: Avatar of Vishnu, challenged Rama

name: Agastya
description: Sage who gave Rama divine weapons

name: Atri
description: Sage whose hermitage Rama visited

name: Anasuya
description: Wife of sage Atri, gave Sita ornaments

name: Guha
description: Nishada chief, friend of Rama

name: Manthara
description: Hunchback maid who poisoned Kaikeyi's mind

name: Khara
description: Demon commander killed by Rama in Dandaka forest

name: Dushana
description: Demon killed by Rama alongside Khara

name: Kabandha
description: Headless demon who directed Rama to Sugriva

name: Shabari
description: Devotee who offered berries to Rama

name: Tara
description: Wife of Vali, wise monkey queen

name: Ruma
description: Wife of Sugriva

name: Swayamprabha
description: Apsara who helped vanaras escape a cave

name: Sushena
description: Vanara physician in Rama's army

name: Malyavan
description: Ravana's grandfather who advised peace

name: Prahasta
description: Ravana's general, killed in battle

name: Aksha
description: Son of Ravana, killed by Hanuman

name: Narada
description: Divine sage who narrated Rama's story to Valmiki

name: Valmiki
description: Author of the Ramayana

name: Brahma
description: Creator god

name: Vishnu
description: Preserver god, Rama is his avatar

name: Shiva
description: God of destruction, owner of the bow Rama broke

name: Indra
description: King of gods

name: Vayu
description: Wind god, father of Hanuman

name: Surya
description: Sun god, grandfather of Sugriva

name: Agni
description: Fire god, tested Sita's purity

name: Yama
description: God of death

name: Garuda
description: Divine eagle, mount of Vishnu

name: Riksharajas
description: Father of Vali and Sugriva

name: Ahalya
description: Sage Gautama's wife, freed from curse by Rama

name: Gautama
description: Sage who cursed Ahalya

name: Lanka
description: Island fortress of Ravana

name: Ayodhya
description: Capital city, Rama's kingdom

name: Mithila
description: Janaka's kingdom

name: Kishkindha
description: Monkey kingdom of Sugriva

name: Dandaka
description: Forest where Rama lived in exile

name: Panchavati
description: Hermitage of Rama in Dandaka forest

name: Chitrakuta
description: Mountain where Rama stayed during exile

name: Rishyamuka
description: Mountain where Sugriva hid from Vali

name: Ashoka Grove
description: Garden in Lanka where Sita was held captive

name: Sarayu
description: River near Ayodhya

name: Ganga
description: Sacred river

name: Brahmastra
description: Supreme weapon of Brahma

name: Agneyastra
description: Fire weapon

name: Pashupatastra
description: Weapon of Shiva

name: Narayanastra
description: Weapon of Vishnu

name: Pinaka
description: Shiva's bow broken by Rama at Sita's swayamvara

name: Pushpaka Vimana
description: Flying chariot originally of Kubera, taken by Ravana
